what_it_does: A prescriptive, unverified proposal to fuse OpenWebUI's branching conversation DAG with the company's session/recall/store stack by adding a new company-owned conversation-content leaf (conversations/*.jsonl), making OWUI a view rather than a parallel store, and wiring the supervisor's reader to append message events.
observations: Claims there are three record-kinds, not two: CC transcript holds content, company agent_sessions holds metadata/pointers, OWUI Chat holds content plus branching DAG.
Proposes .data/store/conversations/<conversation_id>.jsonl as append-only message events with parent_id, children computed by a read-time fold, and a current leaf.
Incremental plan: Inc.1 store module done and verified against a stub store; Inc.2 wire supervisor reader with exact insertion points; Inc.3 re-point recall/recollection to the new leaf.
Verified live recollection split-brain: two sqlite DBs with divergent schemas, absolute archive paths, and the newer schema DB has empty vector tables.
Live OWUI bridge syncs channels only and never touches /api/v1/chats, so conversations are currently unfused.
Requires fail-soft wiring: conversation-store failures must never raise into the supervisor reader/inject path.
States the company currently has no message-content + branching record across agent_sessions, events.jsonl, chat.jsonl, chat_threads, or sessions/.
Lists honest gaps, including unverified OWUI source-line claims and unidentified canonical recollection DB.
standouts: The central argument that the company lacks a conversation-content store, so fusion means building a new leaf rather than choosing between existing stores.
Concrete incremental wiring plan with line-level insertion points in runtime/session_supervisor.py and a fail-soft safety law.
Live-verified inventory of record kinds and a non-destructive resolution path for the recollection split-brain.
conventions: Follows company ledger frontmatter: type proposal, register prescriptive, status unconfirmed, relates-to links.
Uses live verification claims with cited evidence and explicit honest gaps.
Aligns with company FsStore pattern: append-only leaf + read-time fold, graph-locked appends, fsync discipline.
Rejects bridge/sync between two chat stores in favor of one store and OWUI as a view.
Maintains single-writer audit C6 by keeping the supervisor as sole writer to events.jsonl and adding only a secondary leaf.
concerns: Status is unconfirmed and the proposal depends on a new store leaf not yet live in production.
Some OWUI Chat/ChatMessage claims are sourced from a map and not re-verified against the installed OWUI source.
Making OWUI a view may require forking the compiled OWUI chat UI; the fallback of a company-owned chat surface is not detailed.
Recollection convergence depends on identifying the live canonical DB, which is not yet confirmed.
notes: A live-verified proposal to replace dual conversation stores with a single company-owned branching conversation leaf, with a concrete supervisor-wiring plan and a flagged recollection split-brain.
questions: Has runtime/conversations.py been committed and tested in the live repository, or only against a stub store?
Which recollection DB is the live MCP service actually reading?
Are the cited OWUI Chat/ChatMessage line numbers accurate for the installed OWUI version?
What is the exact fallback if the OWUI compiled chat UI cannot be rewired and must remain only a model gateway?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to propose fusing conversations and sessions into one record; actual content is a detailed architectural argument, incremental implementation plan, and verification notes. Match: yes, with extra operational detail.
intention_signals: It tries to prevent a patchwork 'sync two chat stores' solution and instead build a single company-owned branching conversation substrate, harvesting OWUI's DAG shape and UX patterns while keeping the company store as the authoritative log.
novelty: Distinctive for insisting the company currently has no conversation-content store at all, and for providing line-level supervisor insertion points plus a fail-soft law rather than a high-level sync design.
contribution: A concrete one-store branching conversation model, an incremental wiring plan for the supervisor reader, a live-verified inventory of existing record kinds, and a non-destructive resolution path for the recollection split-brain.
summary_for_embedding: A prescriptive proposal to fuse OpenWebUI's branching chat DAG into the company store by adding a conversations/*.jsonl append-only leaf, wiring the supervisor reader to append message events, and re-pointing recall, with live verification that the company currently has no message-content+branching record.
intention_for_embedding: To argue for building a single company-owned conversation substrate rather than bridging two existing chat stores, and to provide an incremental implementation plan with verified insertion points.
